title: machine: on linux we fail to mount the home directoy
body:
Use podman from main and try to start a machine with the default mounts which should mount the $HOME dir,

If that dir is /home it will fail now


```
$ systemctl status home-pholzing.mount 
× home-pholzing.mount - /home/pholzing
     Loaded: loaded (/etc/systemd/system/home-pholzing.mount; enabled; preset: enabled)
     Active: failed (Result: resources)
 Invocation: 2c526293d1cf416a82397629d3287d80
      Where: /home/pholzing
       What: 811b848f5a890021c9a515e8bf5c2e395ef1

Jun 11 10:45:45 localhost systemd[1]: home-pholzing.mount: Mount path /home/pholzing is not canonical (contains a symlink).
Jun 11 10:45:45 localhost systemd[1]: home-pholzing.mount: Failed with result 'resources'.
Jun 11 10:45:45 localhost systemd[1]: Failed to mount home-pholzing.mount - /home/pholzing.
```

/home is a symlink to var/home in coreos so systemd refuses to mount and weants us to use the real path instead.
That means we need extra logic to handle this special case.

cc @inknos

comments.body: Hey team, I was able to reproduce this issue. Indeed on /home dir it fails. I've just created a PR for it, could you please check? #28915 Thank you.
